name: Neural Control and Coordination
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Neural Control and Coordination

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Neural Control and Coordination

questions.question: Nissl’s granules are absent in:
questions.question_raw: Nissl’s granules are absent in:
questions.explanation: Nissl’s grains are mainly present in cyton and dendrite. These granules are rough endoplasmic reticulum with rosettes of the free ribosome, the site for protein synthesis. It is absent in the axon.

questions.question: In the prosencephalon, first, and second ventricles open into the third ventricle by:
questions.question_raw: In the prosencephalon, first, and second ventricles open into the third ventricle by:
questions.explanation: In prosencephalon, the first and second ventricles open into third ventricles by foramen of Monro. It is the opening that connects the left and right sides of the lateral ventricle in the brain.

questions.question: The property which doesn’t belong to nerve fibres is:
questions.question_raw: The property which doesn’t belong to nerve fibres is:
questions.explanation: Nerve fibres are specialized to transmit impulses from one part of the body to others. Nerve fibres do not show the property of elasticity and contractility.

questions.question: Hypothalamus helps to maintain homeostasis by controlling hunger, thirst, blood pressure, temperature feelings, etc. So it is an important link between:
questions.question_raw: Hypothalamus helps to maintain homeostasis by controlling hunger, thirst, blood pressure, temperature feelings, etc. So it is an important link between:
questions.explanation: One of the most important functions of the hypothalamus is to link the nervous system to the endocrine system via the pituitary gland (hypophysis). The hypothalamus is located below the thalamus and is part of the limbic system.

questions.question: Brain stem is the support system of brain and is the collective name for:
questions.question_raw: Brain stem is the support system of brain and is the collective name for:
questions.explanation: The brainstem (or brain stem) is the posterior part of the brain, adjoining and structurally continuous with the spinal cord. In the human brain, the brainstem includes the midbrain, the pons, and the medulla oblongata. Sometimes the diencephalon, the caudal part of the forebrain, is included.

questions.question:
Match the following :
 Column A
 Column B
 a) Unipolar nerves
 i) Nerve cells of retina
 b) Mixed nerves
 ii) Nerve cells of cerebral cortex
 c) bipolar nerves
 iii) Nerves of embryonic stage
 d) Multipolar nerves
 iv) All the spinal nerves
questions.question_raw:
Match the following :
 

Column A Column B
a) Unipolar nerves i) Nerve cells of retina
b) Mixed nerves ii) Nerve cells of cerebral cortex
c) bipolar nerves iii) Nerves of embryonic stage
d) Multipolar nerves iv) All the spinal nerves
questions.explanation: Unipolar nerves are present in nerves of embryonic stage. Mixed nerves are found in all the spinal nerves. Bipolar nerves are present in nerve cells of retina. Multipolarnerves are present in nerve cells of cerebral cortex.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Column A
 Column B
 (a) Motor Neuron
 (i) carry impulse away from cyton
 (b) Axon
 (ii) carry impulse from receptor to CNS
 (c) Afferent Neuron
 (iii) carry impulse towards cyton
 (d) Dendrite
 (iv) carry impulse from CNS to effector
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Column A Column B
(a) Motor Neuron (i) carry impulse away from cyton
(b) Axon (ii) carry impulse from receptor to CNS
(c) Afferent Neuron (iii) carry impulse towards cyton
(d) Dendrite (iv) carry impulse from CNS to effector
questions.explanation: Motor neurons carry impulse from CNS to the effector. Axon carries the impulse away from cyton. Afferent neuron carries impulse from receptor to CNS and dendrite carry impulse towards cyton.

questions.question: Complete the following hierarchical chart showing parts of the nervous system :
questions.question_raw: Complete the following hierarchical chart showing parts of the nervous system :
questions.explanation: The human nervous system consists of the central nervous system and peripheral nervous system. The peripheral nervous system includes 12 pairs of cranial nerves emerging from the brain and 31 pairs of spinal nerves arising from a spinal nerve.

questions.question:
In the following figure a synapse is shown :
i & ii (from up to down)
is a presynaptic membrane and
is a postsynaptic membrane.
synaptic vesicles are in postsynaptic knob.
neurotransmitter receptors are located on the postsynaptic membrane
questions.question_raw:
In the following figure a synapse is shown :

i & ii (from up to down)

  1.  
    1. is a presynaptic membrane and
    2. is a postsynaptic membrane.
  2. synaptic vesicles are in postsynaptic knob.
  3. neurotransmitter receptors are located on the postsynaptic membrane
questions.explanation: The synapse is formed by the membranes of apre-synaptic neuron and a post-synaptic neuron, which may or may not be separated by a gap called synaptic cleft. The neurotransmitter receptors are located on the postsynaptic membrane.

questions.question: Due to refractory period, a nerve fibre in human body on an average transmits about:
questions.question_raw: Due to refractory period, a nerve fibre in human body on an average transmits about:
questions.explanation: The refractory period is the time taken to nerve become ready to conduct other impulses. Due to this refractory period, a nerve fibre in the human body on average transmits about 1000 impulses per second.

questions.question:
Read the following about the surface of cerebral hemispheres:
 Is made up of the pia mater.
 Is greatly convoluted and called the cerebral cortex.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following about the surface of cerebral hemispheres:

  1. Is made up of the pia mater.
  2. Is greatly convoluted and called the cerebral cortex.

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

correct_option_texts: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct
questions.explanation: The layer of cells which covers the cerebral hemisphere is called the cerebral cortex and is thrown into prominent folds.

questions.question:
The autonomic nervous system or ANS:
 Is truly autonomic i.e. independent of the CNS.
 Is also known as the VNS.
 Transmit impulse from CNS to involuntary organs and smooth muscles.
questions.question_raw:
The autonomic nervous system or ANS:

  1. Is truly autonomic i.e. independent of the CNS.
  2. Is also known as the VNS.
  3. Transmit impulse from CNS to involuntary organs and smooth muscles.
questions.explanation: The autonomic nervous system or ANS is the division of the peripheral nervous system that influences the function of internal organs. ANS is also known as VNS. These nerves transmit impulse from CNS to involuntary organs and smooth muscles.

questions.question: Label the arrows in the given diagram of the brain from top to bottom :
questions.question_raw: Label the arrows in the given diagram of the brain from top to bottom :
questions.explanation: The part of the human brain shown above has marked as cerebellum, pons and medulla oblongata of the hindbrain.

questions.question: The band of nerve fibers that joins the two cerebral hemisphere in mammals is:
questions.question_raw: The band of nerve fibers that joins the two cerebral hemisphere in mammals is:
questions.explanation: The corpus callosum (CC) is the largest of the commissural fibres, linking the cerebral cortex of the left and right cerebral hemisphere.

questions.question: Vermis is a portion of:
questions.question_raw: Vermis is a portion of:
questions.explanation: The cerebral vermis is located in the medial, cortico-nuclear zone of the cerebellum. It is constricted between the lobe of the cerebellum.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Neural Control and Coordination

questions.question: Part of brain that acts as relay centre to receive and transmit general sensations except smell:
questions.question_raw: Part of brain that acts as relay centre to receive and transmit general sensations except smell:
questions.explanation: Thalamus is the part of the brain that acts as a relay centre to receive and transmit general sensations except for the smell. Thalamus is located in the forebrain superior to the midbrain.

questions.question: Brain cavities are filled with CSF. In subarachnoid space, the CSF plays the following roles:
questions.question_raw: Brain cavities are filled with CSF. In subarachnoid space, the CSF plays the following roles:

options.label: A
options.text: Acts as a shock proof cushion

options.label: B
options.text: Changes pressure inside cranium in coordination with blood pressure

options.label: C
options.text: Plays a role in night vision

options.label: D
options.text: Keeps brain and spinal cord dry to prevent infection

correct_option_texts: Acts as a shock proof cushion
questions.explanation: Brain cavities are filled with cerebrospinal fluid (CSF). In subarachnoid space, the CSF acts as a shockproof cushion to prevent injury in case of an accident.

questions.question:
In Pavlov’s conditioned reflex experiment find the correct matches :
 Column A
 Column B
 a) conditioned stimulus
 i) salivation in response to food
 b) conditioned response
 ii) food itself
 c) unconditioned stimulus
 iii)sound of the bell
 d) unconditioned response
 iv) salivation in response to bell
questions.question_raw:
In Pavlov’s conditioned reflex experiment find the correct matches :
 

Column A Column B
a) conditioned stimulus i) salivation in response to food
b) conditioned response ii) food itself
c) unconditioned stimulus iii)sound of the bell
d) unconditioned response iv) salivation in response to bell
questions.explanation: There are different kinds of stimulus that is received by different receptors in our body. Hearing of sound of bell is conditioned stimulus and salivation in response to bell is conditioned response. Food itself is conditioned stimulus and salivation in response to food is unconditioned response.

questions.question: In the following identify from up to down :
questions.question_raw: In the following identify from up to down :
questions.explanation: The reflex pathway comprises at least one afferent neuron (receptor) and one efferent (effector excitor) neuron appropriately arranged in a series. The (iii) and (iv) shows gray and white matter.

questions.question: A region on retina of the human eye which is free from rods and cones is:
questions.question_raw: A region on retina of the human eye which is free from rods and cones is:
questions.explanation: The optic nerves leave the eye and the retinal blood vessels enter it at a point medial to and slightly above the posterior pole of the eyeball. Photoreceptor cells are not present in that region and hence it is called the blind spot.

questions.question: The vestibular apparatus is composed of:
questions.question_raw: The vestibular apparatus is composed of:
questions.explanation: The vestibular apparatus is composed of three semicircular canals and the otolith organ consisting of the saccule and utricle.

questions.question: The space within cochlea called scala media is filled with_______.
questions.question_raw: The space within cochlea called scala media is filled with_______.
questions.explanation: Cochlear duct or scala media has endolymph and high potassium ion concentration that the stereocilia of the hair cells protrude into helicotrema.

questions.question: Blood vessels are found to be present in which region of an eye?
questions.question_raw: Blood vessels are found to be present in which region of an eye?
questions.explanation: The middle layer, choroid contains many blood vessels and looks bluish in colour.

questions.question: Sneha ceased to get the smell of a perfume applied to her clothes but her friend Reema who passed by complimented her for the noticeable fragrance. This is an example of:
questions.question_raw: Sneha ceased to get the smell of a perfume applied to her clothes but her friend Reema who passed by complimented her for the noticeable fragrance. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: The sensory organs detect all types of changes in the environment and send appropriate signals to the CNS, where all the inputs are processed and analysed.

questions.question:
Among photoreceptors, cones are responsible for the bright light vision and them also:
 give colour vision because they contain 3 different pigments.
 pigments are sensitive to red, green, and yellow lights.
 pigments are sensitive to red, green, and blue lights.
 individual cone cell contains all the three pigments in it.
questions.question_raw:
Among photoreceptors, cones are responsible for the bright light vision and them also:

  1. give colour vision because they contain 3 different pigments.
  2. pigments are sensitive to red, green, and yellow lights.
  3. pigments are sensitive to red, green, and blue lights.
  4. individual cone cell contains all the three pigments in it.
questions.explanation: In the human eye, there are three types of cones which possess their own characteristic photopigments that respond to red, green, and blue lights. The sensations of different colours are produced by various combinations of these cones and their photopigments.

questions.question: Select the correct terms for labels
questions.question_raw: Select the correct terms for labels
questions.explanation: The external layer is composed of a dense connective tissue and is called the sclera. The ciliary body itself continues forward to form a pigmented and opaque structure called the iris which is the visible coloured portion of the eye. In front of the lens, the aperture surrounded by the iris is called the pupil.

questions.question: In the diagram below, 1 and 2 are
questions.question_raw: In the diagram below, 1 and 2 are

options.label: A
options.text: External auditory canal and Eustachian tube

options.label: B
options.text: Cochlear nerve and external auditory canal

options.label: D
options.text: Eustachian tube and External auditory canal

correct_option_texts: Eustachian tube and External auditory canal
questions.explanation: The outer ear consists of the pinna and external auditory meatus or canal. A Eustachian tube connects the middle ear cavity with the pharynx.

questions.question: The human internal ear is:
questions.question_raw: The human internal ear is:
questions.explanation: The human body is triploblastic in nature, ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm. These three layers of the embryo form the different organs of human beings. The human internal ear is ectodermal in origin.

questions.question: The correctly matched pair is:
questions.question_raw: The correctly matched pair is:

options.label: A
options.text: Birds $\to $ both nocturnal and diurnal vision

questions.explanation: Cats have binocular vision that is cat can use two eyes together. This kind of vision helps in-depth measurement and analysis of the image formed on the retina.

questions.question: In the prosencephalon, first, and second ventricles open into the third ventricle by:
questions.question_raw: In the prosencephalon, first, and second ventricles open into the third ventricle by:
questions.explanation: In prosencephalon, the first and second ventricles open into third ventricles by foramen of Monro. It is the opening that connects the left and right sides of the lateral ventricle in the brain.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Neural Control and Coordination

questions.question: Part of brain that acts as relay centre to receive and transmit general sensations except smell:
questions.question_raw: Part of brain that acts as relay centre to receive and transmit general sensations except smell:
questions.explanation: Thalamus is the part of the brain that acts as a relay centre to receive and transmit general sensations except for the smell. Thalamus is located in the forebrain superior to the midbrain.

questions.question:
Among the following correct matches for ear parts and functions are:
 (a) Cristae
 (i) responsible for lubrication and protection
 (b) Cerumen
 (ii) responsible for dynamic equilibrium
 (c) Organ of Corti
 (iii) responsible for static equilibrium
 (d) Maculae
 (iv) responsible for hearing
questions.question_raw:
Among the following correct matches for ear parts and functions are:

(a) Cristae (i) responsible for lubrication and protection
(b) Cerumen (ii) responsible for dynamic equilibrium
(c) Organ of Corti (iii) responsible for static equilibrium
(d) Maculae (iv) responsible for hearing
questions.explanation: The cristae present in the internal ear is responsible for dynamic equilibrium. Cerumen is responsible for lubrication and protection. The organ of Corti is responsible for hearing and maculae is responsible for the static equilibrium of the body.

questions.question: Gustatoreceptors are responsible for the sense of:
questions.question_raw: Gustatoreceptors are responsible for the sense of:
questions.explanation: Gustatoreceptors are responsible for the sense of taste. Gustatory receptors are located inside taste buds on the tongue.

questions.question: In the process of eye donation, a particular part of a dead man’s eye is stored and grafted on another person’s eye to restore vision. That donated part of an eye is:
questions.question_raw: In the process of eye donation, a particular part of a dead man’s eye is stored and grafted on another person’s eye to restore vision. That donated part of an eye is:
questions.explanation: The eye donation process involves grafting of the cornea of a dead man’s eye, which is stored and grafted on another person’s eye to restore vision.

questions.question:
Read the following about neurohypophysis:
 Neurohypophysis secretes neurohormones.
 Oxytocin and ADH are synthesized in the neurohypophysis.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following about neurohypophysis:

  1. Neurohypophysis secretes neurohormones.
  2. Oxytocin and ADH are synthesized in the neurohypophysis.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: Neurohyphysis secretes neurohormones, which is a posterior gland, itself is a neural projection from the hypothalamus. Neurohypophyis secretes vasopressin and oxytocin hormone.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 (a) Hypophysis
 (i) Sense organs
 (b) Modulator
 (ii) Pituitary
 (c) Receptor
 (iii) Brain and spinal cord
 (d) Effector
 (iv) Muscles and glands
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

(a) Hypophysis (i) Sense organs
(b) Modulator (ii) Pituitary
(c) Receptor (iii) Brain and spinal cord
(d) Effector (iv) Muscles and glands
questions.explanation: The hypophysis is a part of the pituitary gland. The modulator is present in the brain and spinal cord. The receptor is located in sense organs. Effectors are muscles and glands that bring about movement.

questions.question: Find out the mismatched pair:
questions.question_raw: Find out the mismatched pair:
questions.explanation: Different parts of the human brain have a specialized area for different receptors. Visual area is located in the occipital lobe. A general sensory area is located in the parietal lobe, the smell area is located in the frontal area. Speech area is not located in the parietal lobe.

questions.question:
Reticular activating system (RAC) of the brain:
 It works as a bridge to connect the lower brain to the upper brain.
 It helps us to focus on the important things and evade the useless things.
questions.question_raw:
Reticular activating system (RAC) of the brain:

  1. It works as a bridge to connect the lower brain to the upper brain.
  2. It helps us to focus on the important things and evade the useless things.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

questions.explanation: The reticular activating system (RAC) of the brain works as a bridge to connect the lower brain to the upper brain. It helps to focus on the important things and evade useless things.

questions.question:
The choroid layer in the human eye:
 darkens the cavity of the eyeball to prevent internal reflection of light.
 its blood vessels nourish the retina.
 it regulates the shape of the eye lens.
questions.question_raw:
The choroid layer in the human eye:

  1. darkens the cavity of the eyeball to prevent internal reflection of light.
  2. its blood vessels nourish the retina.
  3. it regulates the shape of the eye lens.
questions.explanation: The choroid layer in the human eye darkens the cavity of the eyeball to prevent internal reflection of light. The blood vessels of the choroid layer nourish the retina. The shape of the eye lens is changed by ciliary muscles.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 In the human iris, the contraction of sphincter muscles widens the pupil in dim light to let in more light.
 The amount of pigment in the ciliary body of eye provides an eye with its characteristic colour.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. In the human iris, the contraction of sphincter muscles widens the pupil in dim light to let in more light.
  2. The amount of pigment in the ciliary body of eye provides an eye with its characteristic colour.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

questions.explanation: Human eyes contain iris that is responsible for changing the size of the pupil to allow or prevent the amount of light entering the eyeball. Cons cells present in the retina are responsible for colour perception.

questions.question: While crying, tears come out of eyes but some watery fluid comes out of the nose as well. This fluid is:
questions.question_raw: While crying, tears come out of eyes but some watery fluid comes out of the nose as well. This fluid is:
questions.explanation: While crying, tears come out of eyes. Sometimes water fluid also comes out of the nose as well. This water fluid coming out of the nose is due to excessive secretion of lacrimal glands.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 insects have taste receptors on their feet.
 crickets have ears on the legs.
 snakes are highly sensitive to airborne sounds.
 frogs have colour vision.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. insects have taste receptors on their feet.
  2. crickets have ears on the legs.
  3. snakes are highly sensitive to airborne sounds.
  4. frogs have colour vision.
questions.explanation: Insects have taste receptors on their feet. These receptors are located in different regions to get the stimulus more accurately. Crickets have ears on the legs and frogs have colour vision.

questions.question: Mohan lacks one or more type of cone cells in the retina of his eye. So he will suffer from:
questions.question_raw: Mohan lacks one or more type of cone cells in the retina of his eye. So he will suffer from:
questions.explanation: Colour perception of the human eye is due to the presence of cones in their retina. If one or more kinds of cones is missing from the retina of the eye, the person suffers from a genetic disease called colour blindness.

questions.question:
Match the ear part and their shapes :
 (a) Stapes
 (i) anvil shaped
 (b) External auditory canal
 (ii) hammer-shaped
 (c) Malleus
 (iii) S-shaped
 (d) Incus
 (iv) stir-up shaped
questions.question_raw:
Match the ear part and their shapes :

(a) Stapes (i) anvil shaped
(b) External auditory canal (ii) hammer-shaped
(c) Malleus (iii) S-shaped
(d) Incus (iv) stir-up shaped
questions.explanation: There are three different kinds of bone present in the middle ear of human beings to amplify the sound received by the ear. Stapes are stir-up shaped, the external auditory canal is S-shaped, Malleus is hammer-shaped and the incus is anvil-shaped.

questions.question: Ramesh is suffering from cough and cold. However his taste receptors are working quite well, yet his food do not give him characteristic flavour and is thus less enjoyable because:
questions.question_raw: Ramesh is suffering from cough and cold. However his taste receptors are working quite well, yet his food do not give him characteristic flavour and is thus less enjoyable because:

options.label: A
options.text: Smell also plays a role in tasting food

options.label: B
options.text: Impaired signals of taste are sent due to the spread of viral infection in brain lobes

options.label: D
options.text: Infected smell receptor cells secrete toxins which inhibit the proper working of taste receptors

correct_option_texts: Smell also plays a role in tasting food
questions.explanation: During cough and cold, taste buds work properly but olfactory receptors present in the nose are badly affected. Due to these foods become less enjoyable as smell play a role in tasting food.
